title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: Fork & branch. Create feature branch main (e.g. fix/obs-population-check feature/predict-mode). Make changes. Follow style conventions . Test locally. Run R CMD check test suite pushing (see commands ). Open pull request main clear description changed .

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text:
name imuGAP stands “Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection”. package provides stan-based model estimating vaccination coverage location, cohort, age childhood infectious diseases, measles. core model represents target population life-long propensity vaccination; proportion, ϕ\phi, population unlikely vaccinate complementary proportion, 1−ϕ1 - \phi, likely vaccinate. population experiences vaccination rate, λ\lambda, model time eras, according vaccination eligibility schedule, ν
u. core parameters can vary time location, user-specifiable way. Focusing just core model element, imagine particular population location ii cohort aa (aa denotes start time period group born). cohort now age tt, vaccine schedule first dose ν(t)
u(t), expected fraction group least one dose : P(≥1 dose)=(1−ϕi,)(1−exp{−∫atλi,(s)ν(s)ds}) P(\ge	extrm{1 dose}) = \left(1 - \phi_{, }ight) \left(1 - \exp\left\{-\int_a^{t} \lambda_{, }(s)
u(s) d	extrm{s}ight\}ight) say, representing vaccination coverage via survival-like model.

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: walkthrough demonstrates workflow fitting model predicting coverage simulated data. package includes seven bundled datasets demonstration, representing nested geographic setup (State -> Counties -> Schools).

what: 1. Preparing and Validating the Input Data
title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: First, let’s explore three required input datasets define location hierarchy, observation metadata, actual coverage observations. package provides family canonicalize_* functions validate, clean, convert raw structures canonical forms required sampler. can use directly help troubleshoot inputs, following examples. However, shown next section, sampling() method also automatically canonicalizes inputs.

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: ensure data integrity, canonicalize_* functions enforce strict rules input data format constraints. example, modify observations data number positive cases exceeds total sample size sample_n, validation function raise clear error: Similarly, locations data contains duplicate location IDs, canonicalize_locations() detect duplication throw error:
code: # Create a copy with an invalid observation (positive > sample_n) invalid_obs <- copy(observations_sim) invalid_obs[1, positive := sample_n + 10]  # This will fail validation and throw an error: tryCatch(   canonicalize_observations(invalid_obs),   error = function(e) message("Caught expected error: ", e$message) ) #> Caught expected error: positive must be <= sample_n; found 1 invalid observations with offending ids: 1 # Create a copy with a duplicate location ID invalid_locs <- rbind(   locations_sim,   data.frame(loc_id = "Scruggs", parent_id = "State") )  # This will fail validation: tryCatch(   canonicalize_locations(invalid_locs),   error = function(e) message("Caught expected error: ", e$message) ) #> Caught expected error: locations$loc_id must be unique; found 1 duplicates: 29

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: Using prepared input datasets, can fit Bayesian model using sampling(). options sampler can configured using stan_options(). compiling Stan model running MCMC chain can take time, set eval = FALSE . practice, run: walkthrough, load pre-computed fit object fit_sim bundled package: model fit, can extract posterior draws model parameters using extract_imugap(). example, let’s extract B-spline coefficients representing state-level vaccine uptake baseline: can also look trace plots selected parameters ensure convergence reached.
code: fit_sim <- sampling(   observations_sim,   populations_sim,   locations_sim,   stan_opts = stan_options(     iter = 100,     chains = 1,     refresh = 0,     seed = 1L   ) ) data("fit_sim", package = "imuGAP") beta_draws <- extract_imugap(fit_sim, pars = "beta_bs") str(beta_draws) #> List of 1 #>  $ beta_bs: num [1:2000, 1:5] -1.5 -1.47 -1.52 -1.64 -1.49 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. ..$ iterations: NULL #>   .. ..$           : NULL mcmc_trace(fit_sim$stanfit,   pars = c(     "beta_bs[1]", "beta_bs[2]", "beta_bs[3]",     "beta_bs[4]", "beta_bs[5]"   ) )

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: predict vaccine coverage target population (can include locations cohorts without direct observations, long exist locations hierarchy), first define target grid using create_target(). example, can generate “snapshot” prediction target locations, including State County levels, across ages 1 18:
code: target_sim <- create_target(   fit = fit_sim,   location = locations_sim$loc_id,   age = 1:18,   cohort = max(populations_sim$cohort) - 18,   dose = c(1, 2),   mode = "snapshot" ) head(target_sim) #>    obs_c_id               loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id #>       <int>               <char> <int>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int> #> 1:        1                State     1     30     1      1        1 #> 2:        2              Scruggs     1     30     1      1        2 #> 3:        3               Simone     1     30     1      1        3 #> 4:        4               Watson     1     30     1      1        4 #> 5:        5 Chickadee Elementary     1     30     1      1        8 #> 6:        6     Nuthatch Academy     1     30     1      1       11

title: imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection
text: Finally, run predict() generate predicted coverage probabilities target population combination across posterior draws. Generating predictions also runs Stan model (generated quantities mode) can time-consuming, show code without executing : Instead, load pre-computed prediction results predict_sim bundled package: output predict_sim data.table containing posterior coverage probability predictions (p_obs) MCMC sample (sample_id) target population combination. can summarize predictions get posterior mean credible intervals location, age, dose: Now let’s visualize results. First take look overall state coverage cohort. Note lower coverage among 5 year olds due eligible second dose one year. can also look trend coverage age county level. Note follow trend state differing magnitude. Finally, can look distribution school-level coverage estimates age group. Let’s focus 5-11 year olds ages children elementary schools.

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: functions validate, clean, convert raw user-supplied data structures (locations, observations, populations) canonical forms required [sampling()] sampler underlying Stan models.

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
code: canonicalize_locations(locations)  canonicalize_observations(observations, drop_extra = TRUE)  canonicalize_populations(   populations,   observations,   locations,   max_cohort,   max_age,   max_dose = 2L )

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: locations [data.frame()], columns loc_id parent_id, type. See Details restrictions. observations [data.frame()], observed data, least three columns: obs_id column; type, long unique, non-NA positive column; non-negative integers, observed number vaccinated individuals sample_n column; positive integers, number individuals sampled, must greater equal "positive" optionally, censored column; numeric, NA (uncensored) 1 (right-censored); present, assumed NA drop_extra logical scalar; drop extraneous columns? (default: yes) populations [data.frame()], observation meta data, columns obs_id, type; observation row concerns (.e. id shared observations data object) loc_id, type; location row concerns (.e. id shared locations data object) dose, non-zero, positive integer (1, 2, ...); dose row concerns cohort, positive integer; cohort location row concerns age, positive integer; age cohort row concerns weight, numeric, (0, 1); relative contribution row observation. Optional population row unique obs_id. max_cohort present, maximum cohort present? max_age present, maximum age present? max_dose maximum dose number allow (default: 2L)

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: canonicalize_locations returns data.table, : loc_id, parent_id columns originally supplied, possibly reordered loc_c_id, loc_cp_id columns, canonicalized id/parent_id columns, representing order used sampler layer column, integer 1 (root), 2 (root children), 3 (grandchildren), &c layer_bound column, integer starting 1 layer. provides index slice information used stan model. canonicalize_observations returns canonical observation object, [data.table()] : obs_c_id column, integer sequence 1; order observations passed estimation original obs_id column, possibly reordered positive sample_n columns, possibly reordered "censored" column; NA, present original observations argument canonicalize_populations returns canonical populations object, mirroring input populations, following updates: obs_c_id, observation id row concerns, canonicalized match canonical observation ids loc_c_id, location id row concerns, canonicalized match reordered obs_c_id order

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: imuGAP hierarchical modeling framework requires data structures adhere specific relational format constraints. three canonicalize functions process validate inputs described :

title: Canonicalize imuGAP Data Objects — canonicalize
text: [sampling()] sampler works hierarchical model locations, must provided structure. method checks location structure validity, returns canonical version including layer membership. valid structure : unique root, cycles, duplicate loc_ids Users may explicitly identify root loc_id providing row parent_id equal NA. Otherwise, parent_id appear loc_id treated root. input valid, method create canonicalized version. version, ids run 1:N, N number distinct ids. order determined layer order, position parent within layer, "natural" order (.e., whatever base R sort() yields).

Conceptually, row represents observation vaccination status within population. population need uniform (see [canonicalize_populations()]) concerning single cohort time: observation generally best available resolution data. resolution can vary across rows. sampler uses information resolutions automatically figure compare latent process model different observations. optional censored column: model supports vaccination status indicators vaccine specific well represent individual set vaccines (including target vaccine). specific coverage target vaccine right-censored latter case: full-set-coverage minimum coverage target. least data censored, must supply censored column correctly estimate coverage. title: Create target population for prediction — create_target
text: fit imugap_fit object returned [sampling()] location either vector locations data.frame; vector locations, treated target locations; data.frame, validated target object. age vector ages predict coverage, consistent [canonicalize_populations()] cohort vector cohorts predict coverage, consistent [canonicalize_populations()] dose vector doses predict coverage, consistent [canonicalize_observations()] mode location, age, cohort, dose combined target grid: "error" (default) requires equal-length vectors pairs rowwise; "enumerate" takes combinations; "recycle" recycles least-common-multiple length; "snapshot" takes single reference cohort derives per-age cohorts holding age + cohort constant. See Details.

title: Create target population for prediction — create_target

title: Create target population for prediction — create_target
text: location data.frame, function validates object fit argument. Non-missing values arguments error approach. Otherwise, location must correspond vector location IDs age, cohort, dose must also supplied. Depending mode argument, arguments may different lengths. mode = "error" (default), arguments must length. mode = "enumerate", resulting target combinations arguments. mode = "recycle", resulting target recycle arguments least-common-multiple length. mode = "snapshot", cohort argument must single reference value (represents oldest cohort). resulting target enumerate combinations locations, ages, doses, calculating cohorts age sum age cohort constant (.e., cohort_i = cohort_ref + max_age - age_i). corresponds snapshot time different birth date age combinations.

title: Create target population for prediction — create_target
code: # Load example fit object data("fit_sim")  # 1. Default "error" mode: All vector inputs must have the same length. create_target(   fit = fit_sim,   location = c("Blue Heron School", "Bluebird Learning Center"),   age = c(1, 2),   cohort = c(2, 3),   dose = c(1, 1),   mode = "error" ) #>    obs_c_id                   loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id #>       <int>                   <char> <num>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int> #> 1:        1        Blue Heron School     1      2     1      1        5 #> 2:        2 Bluebird Learning Center     2      3     1      1        6  # Providing mismatched length arguments in "error" mode throws an error: try(create_target(   fit = fit_sim,   location = c("Blue Heron School", "Bluebird Learning Center"),   age = c(1), # length mismatch   cohort = c(2, 3),   dose = c(1, 1),   mode = "error" )) #> Error : All arguments must have the same length in 'error' mode  # 2. "enumerate" mode: Generates all combinations of the arguments. create_target(   fit = fit_sim,   location = c("Blue Heron School", "Bluebird Learning Center"),   age = c(1, 2),   cohort = c(2, 3),   dose = c(1),   mode = "enumerate" ) #>    obs_c_id                   loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id #>       <int>                   <char> <num>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int> #> 1:        1        Blue Heron School     1      2     1      1        5 #> 2:        2 Bluebird Learning Center     1      2     1      1        6 #> 3:        3        Blue Heron School     2      2     1      1        5 #> 4:        4 Bluebird Learning Center     2      2     1      1        6 #> 5:        5        Blue Heron School     1      3     1      1        5 #> 6:        6 Bluebird Learning Center     1      3     1      1        6 #> 7:        7        Blue Heron School     2      3     1      1        5 #> 8:        8 Bluebird Learning Center     2      3     1      1        6  # 3. "recycle" mode: Recycles arguments to the least-common-multiple length. create_target(   fit = fit_sim,   location = c("Blue Heron School", "Bluebird Learning Center"),   age = c(1, 2, 3),   cohort = c(2),   dose = c(1),   mode = "recycle" ) #>    obs_c_id                   loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id #>       <int>                   <char> <num>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int> #> 1:        1        Blue Heron School     1      2     1      1        5 #> 2:        2 Bluebird Learning Center     2      2     1      1        6 #> 3:        3        Blue Heron School     3      2     1      1        5 #> 4:        4 Bluebird Learning Center     1      2     1      1        6 #> 5:        5        Blue Heron School     2      2     1      1        5 #> 6:        6 Bluebird Learning Center     3      2     1      1        6  # 4. "snapshot" mode: Cohort is a single reference value. Cohorts for each # age are calculated so that cohort + age is constant (representing a snapshot in time). create_target(   fit = fit_sim,   location = c("Blue Heron School", "Bluebird Learning Center"),   age = c(1, 2, 3),   cohort = 5,   dose = c(1),   mode = "snapshot" ) #>    obs_c_id                   loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id #>       <int>                   <char> <num>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int> #> 1:        1        Blue Heron School     1      7     1      1        5 #> 2:        2 Bluebird Learning Center     1      7     1      1        6 #> 3:        3        Blue Heron School     2      6     1      1        5 #> 4:        4 Bluebird Learning Center     2      6     1      1        6 #> 5:        5        Blue Heron School     3      5     1      1        5 #> 6:        6 Bluebird Learning Center     3      5     1      1        6  # 5. Providing a data.frame for validation. df_target <- data.frame(   loc_id = c("Blue Heron School", "Bluebird Learning Center"),   age = c(1, 2),   cohort = c(2, 3),   dose = c(1, 1) ) create_target(fit = fit_sim, location = df_target) #>    obs_c_id                   loc_id   age cohort  dose weight loc_c_id #>       <int>                   <char> <num>  <num> <num>  <num>    <int> #> 1:        1        Blue Heron School     1      2     1      1        5 #> 2:        2 Bluebird Learning Center     2      3     1      1        6

# vectorized #> } #>   } #> } #> generated quantities { #>   vector[predict_mode ? n_obs : 0] p_obs; #>   if (predict_mode) { #> vector[n_cohort] logit_phi_st = bs * beta_bs; #> row_vector[n_sch] shift = off_sch; #> for (c in 1:n_cnty) { #>   shift[cnty_map[1,c]:cnty_map[2,c]] += off_cnty[c]; #> } #> vector[(1+ n_cnty + n_sch) * n_cohort] phi = append_row(append_row( #>   inv_logit(logit_phi_st), #>   to_vector(inv_logit( #>     rep_matrix(logit_phi_st, n_cnty) + rep_matrix(off_cnty, n_cohort) #>   ))), #>   to_vector(inv_logit( #>     rep_matrix(logit_phi_st, n_sch) + rep_matrix(shift, n_cohort) #>   )) #> ); #> vector[n_doses * n_yr] unrolled_dose_probs = unrolled_dose(n_yr, n_doses, dose_sched, lambda_raw, epsilon_p); #> vector[n_weights] weighted = (1 - phi[phi_lookup]) .* unrolled_dose_probs[cdf_lookup] .* weights; #> for (obs_i in 1:n_obs) { #>   p_obs[obs_i] = sum(weighted[obs_map[1,obs_i]:obs_map[2,obs_i]]); #> } #>   } #> } #> // This model represents vaccination as a discrete step, fixed hazard process #> // therefore X(t) => X(t + deltaT) = X(t)*exp(-hazard*deltaT) #> // which means the X-out flow == X(t)*(1-exp(-hazard*deltaT)) #> // #> // for an arbitrary number of hazards & deltaTs, we simply sum: #> // net outflow: X(t)*(1-exp(-sum(hazard*deltaT))) #> // #> // This model represents an overall hazard, shared across birth cohorts and #> // vaccine doses. Additionally, birth cohorts have a propensity to vaccinate, #> // which is fixed at birth and then applies throughout life. #> // #> // deltaT captures both time passage + eligibility. deltaT in the fitting is #> // always == 1, but note that this is unitless - the data define what time means #> // e.g. if birth cohorts are based on years, then deltaT is years, and a weight #> // of 1 means eligible for a whole year, .25 eligible for a quarter, etc #> // #> // the hazard is constant for any given discrete time step, but can vary between #> // time steps. the hazard time is denoted in absolute model time. however, #> // cohort time is relative. so: #> // hazard time == 1: one deltaT has passed, absolute time is 1 #> // cohort 1, time 1: one deltaT has passed => cohort 1 is 1 deltaT old, #> //   absolute time is also 1 (cohort 1 is the first cohort) #> // cohort 2, time 2: two deltaT have passed => cohort 2 is 2 deltaT old, but #> //   cohort 1 is 3 deltaT old, and absolute time is 3 #> // generally: #> // cohort n, time t: experienced t deltaTs; title: imuGAP 0.1.0
text: First public release. Initial feature set: sampling(): fits imuGAP Bayesian hierarchical coverage model via rstan::sampling() returns imugap_fit object wrapping underlying stanfit together model settings dataset metadata. predict.imugap_fit(): posterior-predicts coverage probabilities user-supplied target population grid using rstan::gqs(). extract_imugap(): convenience wrapper around rstan::extract() pulling common imuGAP parameters (defaults state-level B-spline coefficients beta_bs). canonicalize_locations(), canonicalize_observations(), canonicalize_populations(): validate convert user-supplied data canonical forms required sampler. imugap_options(): configures model-side settings (B-spline degrees freedom, dose schedule, model object). stan_options(): configures Stan sampler settings (iter, chains, seed, etc.) input validation. impute_school_coverage_process_v6 (adds county- school-level random effects; current default). Bundled example datasets end--end examples tests: locations_sim, observations_sim, populations_sim, reference fit_sim stanfit fixture. pkgdown documentation site published https://accidda.github.io/imuGAP/.
